---
title: "SMARTX: Porewater pH"
author: "May 2025 Samples"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true
output_dir: "To Be Reviewed/PDF"
---

\newpage

##Run Information 
```{r run information, include=TRUE}
cat("Run Information:  ") #lets you know what section you're in 
#set the run date & user name 
  sample_year <- "2025"
  sample_month <- "May"
  user <- "Victoria Ellis"
  
#identify the files you want to read in 
  #read in as a list to accommodate multiple runs in a month
  pH_file <- "Raw Data/1_CO2xCommunity_pH_JULY.csv"

# Define the file path for  file - check year and month 
  final_path <- "Processed Data/GCReW_CO2xCOMM_Porewater_pH_202507.csv"

#record any notes about the run or anything other info here: 
  run_notes <- ""
  
  #Collection_Dates = "Raw Data/Sample_Collection_Dates_2024.csv"

  cat(run_notes)
```

##Setup
```{r setup, include=FALSE}

#let you know which section you are in 
cat("Setup")

#load/install packages
if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  dplyr,
  purrr,
  ggplot2,
  ggpubr,
  tidyr,
  tidyverse,
  lubridate,
  tinytex,
  stringr,
  writexl,
  readr,
  readxl,
  purrr,
  tinytex,
  broom,
  here)

#Set up file path for metadata 
  #downloaded metadata csv - downloaded from Google drive as csv for this year
  #Raw_Metadata = "Raw Data/Sample_IDs_LTREB_SMARTX_2024.csv"
  #Raw_Metadata = "..GCReW/GCReW_Project_Treatment_Metadata.csv"

  Raw_Metadata = here(".." , "GCReW", "GCReW_Project_Treatment_Metadata.csv")
  

```

##Read in metadata and create similar sample IDs for matching to samples 
##Read in metadata and create similar sample IDs for matching to samples 
```{r pull in metadata for later, include=FALSE}

#read in metadata

raw_metadata <- read.csv(Raw_Metadata)
#clean up columns in metadata file:
#Pull out only 1 Meter Rows
#Create a Sample_Name column to match the SEAL
metadata <- raw_metadata %>%
  filter(grepl("1-Meter", Nickname, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(Sample_Name = paste0(Sample.ID)) %>%
  mutate(Month = sample_month) %>%
  mutate(Year = sample_year) %>%
  rename(
    Sample_ID = Sample.ID,
    Plot_Name = Plot,
  )

#Bring in the collection date and any field notes if relevant
#collection_metadata <- read.csv(Collection_Dates)
#then we will merge the collection date for project based on the project and the chambers

```

```{r Import Data, include=FALSE}
cat("Import Data")

#set file path for data - in run info chunk 
path <- ("file path")

#Read in Raw Data 
data_pH <- map(pH_file, read.csv)

# Combining Files
df_combo <- bind_rows(data_pH)

# Select needed columns & rename 
df_all <- df_combo %>%
  mutate(Sample_Name = paste0(Plot, "-",Depth)) %>%
  mutate(Analysis_rundate = paste0(match(Month, month.name), "/", Day, "/", Year)) %>%
select(
         Sample_Name = Sample_Name,
         pH = pH, 
         Analysis_rundate = Analysis_rundate,
       )

#Checking column headers
head(df)




```


## Merge samples with Metadata & check all samples are present 
```{r check sample ids with metadata, echo=FALSE}

cat("Merge Samples with Metadata")

#check to see if all samples are present in the metadata 
all_present <- all(metadata$Sample_Name %in% df_all$Sample_Name)

if (all_present) {
  message("All sample IDs are present in data.")
} else {
  message("Some sample IDs are missing from data.")
  
  # Optional: Which ones are missing?
  missing_ids <- setdiff(metadata$Sample_Name, df_all$Sample_Name)
  print(missing_ids)
}


#merge metadata with sample run data 
merged_data <- df_all %>%
  left_join(metadata, by = c("Sample_Name" = "Sample_Name"))

#might want to include a flag or something to see that all the samples are included? 

df_all_clean <- merged_data 

```

\newpage

##Export Data
```{r, include=FALSE}
#pull out what we need

#check results
#head(Samples)

#pull out what we need

df_all_clean_cols <- df_all_clean %>%
  select(
    Site,
    Project,
    Year,
    Month,
    Community,
    Nickname,
    Plot_Name,
    Depth_cm,
    CO2_Treatment,
    N_Treatment,
    pH
  )

final_data_labeled <- df_all_clean_cols %>%
  mutate(
    Run_notes = run_notes # new column with notes about the run
  )

#rename columns
org_labeled_fin_data <- final_data_labeled %>%
  rename(
    Plot = Plot_Name
  )

#Order Columns for Final Data Frame
All_Clean_Data <- org_labeled_fin_data %>%
  select(
    Site,
    Project,
    Year,
    Month,
    Community,
    Nickname,
    Plot,
    Depth_cm,
    CO2_Treatment,
    N_Treatment,
    pH,
    Run_notes
  )
#head(Samples1)
```


## Visualize Data
```{r Visualize Data, echo=FALSE}
cat("Visualize Data")

### Ammonia 
pH_forplot <- All_Clean_Data

#need to work out how best to plot these data 

 pH_forplot <- pH_forplot %>%
  mutate(Treatment = ifelse(
    CO2_Treatment == "Elevated" &
      Community == "SPPA",
    "eCO2_SPPA",
    ifelse(
      CO2_Treatment == "Elevated" &
        Community == "SCAM",
      "eCO2_SCAM",
      ifelse(CO2_Treatment == "Ambient" &
               Community == "SPPA"  , "aCO2_SPPA" , "aCO2_SCAM")
    )
  )) %>%
  mutate(CO2_Treatment = ifelse(CO2_Treatment == "Ambient", "aCO2", "eCO2")) %>%
  mutate(Order = ifelse(CO2_Treatment == "Ambient", "1", "2"))
 
 pH_forplot <- pH_forplot[order(pH_forplot$CO2_Treatment), ]
 
viz_pH_plot2 <- ggplot(data = pH_forplot, aes(
  x =  factor(as.factor(CO2_Treatment), levels = c("eCO2", "aCO2")), 
  y = pH,
  color = as.factor(Treatment),
  fill = as.factor(Treatment)
)) +
  geom_bar(
    stat = "identity",
    position = position_dodge2(padding = 0.5, preserve = "single")
  ) +
  facet_grid(Depth_cm ~ ., scales = "fixed", space = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
    "eCO2_SPPA" = "#EAC435",
    "eCO2_SCAM" = "#149911", 
    "aCO2_SPPA" = "white",
    "aCO2_SCAM" = "white"
  ),
    name = "Treatment:",
    breaks = c("eCO2_SPPA", "eCO2_SCAM", "aCO2_SPPA", "aCO2_SCAM"),
    labels = c("eCO2_SPPA", "eCO2_SCAM", "aCO2_SPPA", "aCO2_SCAM") 
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "eCO2_SPPA" = "#EAC435",
    "eCO2_SCAM" = "#149911", 
    "aCO2_SPPA" = "#EAC435",
    "aCO2_SCAM" = "#149911"
  ),
    name = "Treatment:",
    breaks = c("eCO2_SPPA", "eCO2_SCAM", "aCO2_SPPA", "aCO2_SCAM"),
    labels = c("eCO2_SPPA", "eCO2_SCAM", "aCO2_SPPA", "aCO2_SCAM") 
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = " ",
       y = "pH (uM)",
       title = "CO2xCOMM: Porewater Methane",
       fill = "CO2 Treatment:",
       color = "Community:") +
  theme(legend.position = "right") +
  scale_x_discrete(drop = TRUE) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(
      color = "black",
      fill = NA,
      linewidth = 0.8
    )
  ) +
coord_flip(ylim = c(5.5,7.1))
print(viz_pH_plot2)


```
```{r, echo = FALSE}
#ensure points are in correct order
pH_forplot <- pH_forplot[order(pH_forplot$Depth_cm), ]

color1 <- c("SC1", "SC2", "SP1", "SP2")
color2 <- c("SC6", "SC4", "SP4", "SP5")
color3 <- c("SC9", "SC8", "SP8", "SP9")
color4 <- c("SC10", "SC12", "SP10", "SP11")
color5 <- c("SC14", "SC15", "SP14", "SP13")

pH_forplot <- pH_forplot %>%
  mutate(
    Plot_Color = case_when(
      Plot %in% color1 ~ "Color1",
      Plot %in% color2 ~ "Color2",
      Plot %in% color3 ~ "Color3",
      Plot %in% color4 ~ "Color4",
      Plot %in% color5 ~ "Color5"
    )
  )

#make plot
viz_pH_plot_2 <- ggplot(data = pH_forplot, aes(x = pH, y = as.numeric(Depth_cm), color = Plot_Color)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_path(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Color1" = "#FFD500",
      "Color2" = "#00D0FF",
      "Color3" = "#FF0075",
      "Color4" = "#80d280",
      "Color5" = "#ff6040"
    ),
    labels = c("1", "2", "3", "4", "5")
  ) +
  facet_grid(Treatment ~ ., scales = "fixed") +
  labs(
    x = "pH (uM)",
    y = "Depth (cm)",
    title = "CO2xCOMM: Porewater pH by Replicate",
    color = "Replicate"
  ) +
  scale_y_reverse(breaks = c(20, 40, 80), limits = c(90, 10)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(
      color = "black",
      fill = NA,
      linewidth = 0.8
    )
  )

viz_pH_plot_2
```



#Export Data

```{r Export Data, echo = FALSE}
write.csv(All_Clean_Data, final_path)
```
